automotive-ev-battery.title: $6.5bn electric bus push for New South Wales
automotive-ev-battery.summary: New South Wales has announced a major $6.5 billion AUD budget allocation over ten years to purchase 1,700 new electric buses and construct 17 charging depots. This represents one of the largest public transit electrification commitments in the Asia-Pacific region and signals strong government support for sustainable public transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Zenobe secures €243 million for electrified public transport in Australasia
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Fleet electrification firm Zenobe has secured a $400 million AUD financing facility to support the deployment of electric commercial vehicles across Australia and New Zealand. The funding establishes the region's first integrated, multi-site heavy vehicle fleet electrification financing platform.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BEVs over Hydrogen: South Korea considers reallocating subsidies
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The South Korean government is considering redirecting subsidies allocated for hydrogen vehicles and filling stations toward battery electric vehicle incentives instead. This policy shift would strengthen the EV incentive program, which is projected to exhaust its budget by August without additional funding.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EV incentives unveiled in Bangladesh as BYD eyes expansion
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Bangladesh has announced a comprehensive package of tax incentives designed to accelerate EV adoption, including tax breaks for importers, manufacturers, and buyers of battery electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles. The incentives also cover machinery imports and charging station infrastructure, supporting BYD's expansion plans in the market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Solaris expands annual capacity by 500 buses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Polish bus manufacturer Solaris has opened a new production facility near Poznań with over 7,000 square meters dedicated to final assembly of city buses. The expansion will increase the company's annual production capacity by 500 vehicles, supporting growing demand for electric transit solutions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Car subscription provider Finn raises €140 million in funding
automotive-ev-battery.summary: European car subscription platform Finn has completed a financing round totaling approximately €140 million, achieving unicorn status with a valuation exceeding one billion euros. The company is increasingly specializing in electric vehicle offerings within its subscription service.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Zijin invests in proprietary battery-electric mining dump trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese mining company Zijin Mining is advancing fleet electrification by deploying battery-electric dump trucks across multiple operational sites and developing some vehicles in-house. The investment demonstrates growing adoption of commercial EV solutions in the mining and heavy industry sectors.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Stuttgart welcomes four electric bin lorries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz Trucks has delivered the first five of ten eEconic electric refuse vehicles to Stuttgart's waste management authority for operation at a newly established depot. The deployment demonstrates the viability of electric commercial vehicles in challenging urban environments with demanding topography.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rivian drops entry-level R1T and R1S as focus shifts to R2
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Rivian has discontinued the lowest-priced configurations of its R1T pickup and R1S SUV models from its online configurator, signaling a strategic pivot toward higher-margin vehicles. The company is realigning its portfolio ahead of the upcoming R2 crossover launch.

automotive-ev-battery.title: VW software firm Cariad opens AI Hub in Berlin
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen's software subsidiary Cariad has consolidated teams from multiple locations into a centralized 'Automotive Software Campus' in Berlin. The consolidation supports Cariad's cost-reduction initiatives while strengthening its capabilities in automotive software development.

science-space.title: Did NASA just find evidence of ancient life on Mars? Perseverance rover spots complex carbon in Red Planet rocks
science-space.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has detected hundreds of organic compounds in Martian mudstones, representing the most robust organic detection in Jezero crater to date. These complex carbon measurements suggest the presence of conditions that may have supported ancient microbial life on Mars.

science-space.title: NASA's TESS Mission Reveals the 'Puffiest' Planets Ever Found
science-space.summary: NASA has discovered two unprecedented 'super-puff' planets with densities comparable to cotton candy, making them the least dense giant planets ever detected. These Jupiter-sized worlds challenge existing models of planetary formation and composition.

science-space.title: This is the largest and most detailed image of our Milky Way — with over 60 million stars and 50 exoplanet systems
science-space.summary: The European Space Agency's Euclid spacecraft has captured the largest and most detailed image of the Milky Way's heart, containing over 60 million stars and data on 50 exoplanet systems. This breakthrough observation will provide critical data for NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's upcoming core survey.

science-space.title: NASA's aging infrastructure can't handle Artemis launches without $1 billion in upgrades, watchdog warns
science-space.summary: A government watchdog has warned that NASA's aging infrastructure requires approximately $1 billion in upgrades to support planned Artemis lunar missions and commercial space industry expansion. The infrastructure deficiencies present a significant obstacle to NASA's timeline for returning astronauts to the Moon.

science-space.title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA's Moon to Mars Path
science-space.summary: Seven weeks after the Artemis II mission successfully returned four astronauts from a crewed journey around the Moon, NASA officials are analyzing mission achievements to refine plans for lunar surface exploration and future Mars missions. The Orion spacecraft's performance is providing critical data for NASA's Moon to Mars strategy.

science-space.title: NASA to Share Latest Moon Base Mission Progress
science-space.summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will announce updates to the agency's lunar base construction plans during a virtual conference on June 30, including new awards for lunar lander missions. The briefing will preview upcoming development phases in the establishment of a permanent Moon surface outpost.

science-space.title: NASA looks for the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope is conducting observations to determine the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, providing insights into the composition and history of objects traveling through space from other solar systems. This research expands understanding of interstellar material and the broader cosmos.

science-space.title: Euclid View of Milky Way Heart Previews Core Survey by NASA's Roman
science-space.summary: ESA's Euclid spacecraft has provided a detailed preview of the Galactic Center region that NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study in depth. The overlapping observations will accelerate astronomical research by providing complementary datasets on this crucial region of the galaxy.

science-space.title: NASA's HiRISE Captures Perseverance Marking a Milestone on Mars
science-space.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has traveled a full marathon distance (26.2 miles) across the Martian surface after five years and four months of operation. The milestone was documented by orbital imagery from NASA's HiRISE camera, demonstrating the rover's durability and continued scientific productivity.

science-space.title: Roman Telescope Comes to Kennedy
science-space.summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center aboard the Pegasus barge for final processing ahead of its targeted launch in August 2026. The observatory will undergo hazardous servicing and integration procedures before deployment.

world-news.title: Iran war live: US vows to defend Gulf interests; Israel kills 2 in Lebanon
world-news.summary: The United States has pledged to defend its interests in the Persian Gulf amid escalating tensions with Iran, while Israel reported military operations resulting in casualties in southern Lebanon. The situation represents a significant escalation in regional hostilities with implications for global energy markets and international security.

world-news.title: Trump slams NATO over lax participation in Iran war in talk with Mark Rutte
world-news.summary: Former President Trump criticized NATO allies for insufficient participation in what he characterized as an Iran war during discussions with NATO chief Mark Rutte. The dispute underscores growing friction between the United States and Europe over burden-sharing in Middle Eastern military operations.

world-news.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
world-news.summary: Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, announced that nuclear inspectors will visit Iranian nuclear sites as part of ongoing diplomatic negotiations. The development represents a potential breakthrough in international efforts to verify Iran's compliance with nuclear agreements amid regional tensions.

world-news.title: Venezuela struck by back-to-back earthquakes, high casualties feared
world-news.summary: Venezuela was hit by consecutive powerful earthquakes with magnitudes reaching 7.5, causing widespread building collapses in Caracas and significant casualties. The natural disaster threatens humanitarian infrastructure in a nation already facing severe economic and political crisis.

world-news.title: France, UK and Spain see record temperatures as heatwave grips western Europe
world-news.summary: Western Europe is experiencing a severe heatwave with record-breaking temperatures affecting tens of millions of people across France, the United Kingdom, and Spain. Red heat alerts have been issued across the continent as climate change continues to drive extreme weather patterns.

world-news.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
world-news.summary: Sevastopol, Crimea's largest city, experienced widespread power outages following Ukrainian military strikes, according to the Moscow-installed governor. The attack represents a continued escalation in the ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia.

world-news.title: France confirms first Ebola case
world-news.summary: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola virus disease in a doctor who had been working in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where over 260 deaths have been recorded. The case raises international public health concerns regarding disease transmission and outbreak containment.

world-news.title: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
world-news.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda, Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate, conceded defeat to right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella in an extremely close election decided by less than one percentage point. The result represents a significant political shift in Latin America's geopolitical landscape.

world-news.title: Russian opposition leader jailed over anti-war social media posts
world-news.summary: A Russian opposition leader has been imprisoned for publishing anti-war content on social media, reflecting the Kremlin's intensified suppression of dissent. The action underscores continued restrictions on freedom of expression within Russia during the ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Two years on, Kenyan families still seek justice after Gen Z protests
world-news.summary: Families of victims from Kenya's Gen Z-led protests remain without answers as investigations into deaths and disappearances have stalled, with accountability remaining elusive. The continued lack of justice raises concerns about government transparency and human rights enforcement in East Africa.

finance-markets.title: Micron posts 15-fold profit surge as AI companies clamour for chips
finance-markets.summary: Micron Technology reported a dramatic 15-fold increase in profits, with shares rallying in after-hours trading as the chipmaker forecasts sustained demand for computer memory from artificial intelligence companies. The strong results and outlook signal robust continued momentum in the AI infrastructure investment cycle.

finance-markets.title: Qualcomm reveals Meta as first Big Tech customer for data centre chips
finance-markets.summary: Qualcomm announced Meta as its first major technology customer for data centre chips, coupled with a raised revenue outlook, sending shares up as much as 15 percent. The partnership signals growing demand for specialized semiconductors supporting artificial intelligence infrastructure at major technology companies.

finance-markets.title: SK Hynix Surges 12% as US Listing Plan Boosts Valuation Hopes
finance-markets.summary: SK Hynix shares surged 12 percent following the memory chipmaker's announcement of plans for a $29 billion US listing, which analysts believe could enhance valuations through capacity expansion and increased foreign investor access. The listing represents a significant move to raise capital for growth in the semiconductor sector.

finance-markets.title: US banks would lose $700bn in economic crash, Fed stress tests find
finance-markets.summary: Federal Reserve stress tests reveal that major Wall Street banks would face cumulative losses of $700 billion in a severe economic downturn scenario, though several lenders passed the tests and proceeded to boost investor payouts. The results underscore both the financial system's resilience and the scale of potential risks in an adverse economic environment.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of obtaining 'illicit' access to Claude
finance-markets.summary: Anthropic has accused Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba of conducting a large-scale effort to illicitly access its Claude artificial intelligence model through thousands of fraudulent accounts. The allegation highlights growing tensions over AI intellectual property protection and illicit access to proprietary models by foreign competitors.

finance-markets.title: PBOC Plans Overnight Reverse Repo in Next Stage of Policy Shift
finance-markets.summary: China's central bank is introducing an overnight tenor to its open-market operations as part of a broader policy shift aimed at reshaping how it influences short-term borrowing costs. The move represents a significant refinement of the People's Bank of China's monetary policy toolkit.

finance-markets.title: China Starts Marketing Record €5 Billion of Sovereign Bonds
finance-markets.summary: China has begun marketing up to €5 billion in sovereign bonds, potentially representing its largest-ever euro-denominated bond issuance and signaling efforts to diversify its funding sources. The bond offering highlights Beijing's continued access to international capital markets.

finance-markets.title: Japan's Household Assets Rise to ¥2,386 Trillion at End of March
finance-markets.summary: Japan's household assets increased 7.1 percent to ¥2.386 trillion ($14.7 trillion) at the end of March, reaching the second-highest level on record according to Bank of Japan data. The rise reflects strength in financial and real assets among Japanese households despite demographic headwinds.

finance-markets.title: Bitcoin hits 20-month low as market sentiment sours
finance-markets.summary: Bitcoin has fallen below $60,000 to hit a 20-month low as market sentiment deteriorates and retail investors shift capital toward artificial intelligence-related stocks and equities. The decline reflects a broadening reallocation of speculative capital within growth-oriented asset classes.

finance-markets.title: Brent Oil Erases Wartime Gains as Hormuz Reopening Boosts Supply
finance-markets.summary: Brent crude oil has surrendered all wartime-driven gains as increased flows through the Strait of Hormuz following progress on a US-Iran peace deal boost global supply availability. The price retreat reflects normalized geopolitical risk premiums and improved energy market equilibrium.

tech-ai.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
tech-ai.summary: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are departing Google to join Anthropic, continuing a trend of senior talent exodus from the search giant. This follows previous departures of prominent scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper, signaling ongoing competitive pressure in the AI sector.

tech-ai.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington's chip war
tech-ai.summary: Europe is challenging U.S. semiconductor restrictions targeting China, with ASML's CEO noting that proposed export controls like the MATCH Act would block older-generation technology that has been in circulation for a decade. The dispute highlights growing transatlantic tensions over chip supply chain controls.

tech-ai.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
tech-ai.summary: Cerebras Technologies saw its stock price decline sharply following its first earnings report as a public company, after forecasting narrower gross margins in its core AI chipmaking business. The margin guidance spooked investors concerned about the company's profitability trajectory in the competitive AI hardware market.

tech-ai.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
tech-ai.summary: A U.S. memory chip manufacturer posted exceptional results, with revenue quadrupling to $41.45 billion year-over-year, while profits surged from $1.88 billion to $28.2 billion. The dramatic growth reflects strong market demand capitalizing on ongoing semiconductor supply constraints.

tech-ai.title: A new paper argues Microsoft exaggerated its quantum claims a year ago
tech-ai.summary: A peer-reviewed critique published in Nature questions the validity of Microsoft's topological qubit technology featured in its Majorana 1 processor, which the company unveiled in February 2025 as a quantum computing breakthrough. The paper challenges the fundamental science behind Microsoft's quantum computing claims and its successor Majorana 2 chip.

tech-ai.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they're the most resilient
tech-ai.summary: Despite widespread predictions that artificial intelligence would eliminate engineering positions, new data from SignalFire indicates that engineers comprise a larger share of hiring than previously expected. The finding contradicts the narrative that AI-driven layoffs are predominantly affecting technical roles.

tech-ai.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
tech-ai.summary: Vishal Sikka, former Infosys CEO, has launched a new venture backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, assembling experienced talent from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI. The startup aims to disrupt the traditional IT services sector with a new competitive approach.

tech-ai.title: Google is finally opening the Play Store to outside payments
tech-ai.summary: Google has announced plans to implement alternative billing systems across its Play Store globally, replacing its flat 30 percent fee with lower, decoupled fees following the Epic Games antitrust settlement. The changes represent a significant shift in how developers pay commissions on Android app transactions.

tech-ai.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
tech-ai.summary: Organizations are implementing token rationing measures as employees increasingly use AI systems for minor tasks that deplete budgets quickly, signaling an end to the earlier era of unconstrained token spending. The shift reflects growing concerns about AI cost control and efficiency in enterprise deployments.

tech-ai.title: New website names and shames companies that still don't offer passkeys to users
tech-ai.summary: A new public accountability website highlights that 24 percent of the world's most popular websites have yet to implement passkey support, the security industry's preferred authentication method. The initiative aims to pressure major platforms to adopt the more secure login technology.

health-biotech.title: FDA backs Gilead's Trodelvy for first-line, triple-negative breast cancer
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has granted Gilead two approvals for its Trodelvy therapy in first-line triple-negative breast cancer treatment, expanding the drug's commercial opportunity one month after a competing therapy from Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca received similar approval. This represents a significant expansion of Trodelvy's indicated use in a major cancer indication.

health-biotech.title: Wagering on FDA changes, Regenxbio will submit Duchenne gene therapy for approval
health-biotech.summary: Regenxbio announced plans to submit its Duchenne muscular dystrophy gene therapy for FDA approval, proceeding with an accelerated approval strategy just one month after regulators requested an additional trial. This move represents a significant development in gene therapy treatment for the serious genetic disorder.

health-biotech.title: Ionis wins FDA label expansion to reduce triglycerides, pancreatitis
health-biotech.summary: Ionis received FDA approval to expand its Tryngolza antisense drug label to treat patients with severely elevated triglyceride levels, significantly broadening the drug's commercial opportunity. The approval reflects the growing clinical utility of antisense oligonucleotide technology in metabolic disease treatment.

health-biotech.title: Clinical trial set to test two drugs for fast-growing Ebola outbreak
health-biotech.summary: A clinical trial testing two experimental drugs against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus is set to launch next week to address a rapidly spreading outbreak in Central Africa, with WHO officials overseeing the initiative. The trial represents a critical public health response to contain the infectious disease emergency.

health-biotech.title: How GSK's new CEO lined up a major buyout to start his time at the top
health-biotech.summary: GSK's new CEO Luke Miels orchestrated a $10.6 billion acquisition of Nuvalent to significantly strengthen the company's oncology portfolio early in his tenure. The strategic buyout underscores the pharmaceutical giant's commitment to expanding its cancer drug capabilities.

health-biotech.title: AI wades into a vexing medical mystery: What causes sudden cardiac death?
health-biotech.summary: A new Nature study employs artificial intelligence to identify individuals at high risk for sudden cardiac death and uncover potential underlying causes of this critical condition. The research demonstrates the emerging clinical potential of AI-driven analytics in cardiovascular disease prevention.

health-biotech.title: Novartis taps Antares in small molecule discovery pact with $105M upfront
health-biotech.summary: Novartis partnered with Antares Therapeutics to discover novel small molecule candidates targeting previously undruggable cancer targets, with Antares receiving $105 million upfront. The collaboration aims to unlock new therapeutic opportunities in oncology through innovative small molecule chemistry.

health-biotech.title: U.S. health spending rose sharply in 2025, thanks to GLP-1 use and more care
health-biotech.summary: U.S. healthcare spending increased substantially in 2025, driven by widespread adoption of GLP-1 drugs and expanded utilization of medical services across the health system. The spending surge reflects the significant economic impact of next-generation obesity and diabetes medications on overall healthcare costs.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly dives into hair loss treatments with investment in AI startup Absci
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly invested in biotech startup Absci to develop novel hair growth medications and potentially treat endometriosis, leveraging artificial intelligence-driven drug discovery. The investment reflects major pharmaceutical companies' growing focus on aesthetics and rare gynecological conditions.

health-biotech.title: Former employee sues and claims BioNTech cut corners in pancreatic cancer trials
health-biotech.summary: A former senior clinical trial manager at BioNTech filed a lawsuit alleging protocol deviations and safety concerns in the company's ongoing pancreatic cancer trials. The legal action raises questions about trial conduct and regulatory compliance at one of the industry's high-profile vaccine developers.

crypto-web3.title: Binance withdraws Greek MiCA bid but vows to remain in Europe
crypto-web3.summary: Binance has withdrawn its application for a license in Greece but maintains commitment to securing regulatory approval elsewhere in the European Union before the July 1 deadline. The crypto exchange faces mandatory operational shutdown across the EU if it fails to obtain proper licensing before the regulatory cutoff date.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin miners face deepening margin squeeze as revenue falls below production costs
crypto-web3.summary: Approximately 20% of Bitcoin miners are currently operating at a loss as revenue has declined below production costs amid market headwinds. Network-level stress indicators reflect the severe profitability crisis affecting a significant portion of the mining ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: Trump's refusal to sign housing bill could delay Congress and imperil Clarity Act
crypto-web3.summary: President Trump declined to sign the bipartisan housing bill containing a CBDC prohibition provision, potentially disrupting legislative momentum on crypto-related policy. The action threatens passage of the Clarity Act and other pending cryptocurrency regulation.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi targets a massive $40 billion valuation, widening lead over rival Polymarket
crypto-web3.summary: Prediction market operator Kalshi is pursuing a funding round that could value the company at $40 billion, further extending its competitive advantage over Polymarket. The firm is reportedly planning a potential public listing in 2027.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi sues Illinois, Pritzker over state bill implementing prediction markets regulatory regime
crypto-web3.summary: Kalshi has filed legal action against the State of Illinois and Governor Pritzker challenging the state's newly enacted prediction markets regulatory framework. The lawsuit represents escalating tensions between crypto platforms and states seeking to establish independent regulatory authority.

crypto-web3.title: Upheaval at the Ethereum Foundation has some of crypto's biggest names feeling bullish
crypto-web3.summary: The Ethereum Foundation experienced significant organizational changes this week alongside the launch of EthLabs, generating positive sentiment among prominent figures in the crypto community. The developments suggest potential shifts in governance and strategic direction for the leading smart contract platform.

crypto-web3.title: SecondFi loses $2.4 million in Cardano wallet exploit
crypto-web3.summary: SecondFi suffered losses of $2.4 million from three coordinated security exploits targeting flaws in its wallet generation software. The platform's rapid response prevented approximately $129 million in additional ADA holdings from being compromised.

crypto-web3.title: World Cup boom sends Polymarket volume up 300% while Kalshi sets open interest records
crypto-web3.summary: Sports betting demand surrounding the World Cup has driven Polymarket trading volume up 300%, while competitor Kalshi simultaneously achieved a record $1.16 billion in aggregated open interest. The milestone marks the first instance of Kalshi's open interest surpassing the billion-dollar threshold.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 as AI trade continues to draw investor interest and capital
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin has declined below the $60,000 threshold as institutional capital continues flowing toward artificial intelligence-related investments and equity offerings. South Korean semiconductor manufacturers are simultaneously raising billions in concurrent U.S. equity offerings.

crypto-web3.title: Who gets a direct line to the Fed? Congress weighs risks of Fed 'skinny accounts' for crypto and fintech firms
crypto-web3.summary: Congress is debating the appropriate scope of Federal Reserve access for cryptocurrency and fintech companies under proposed 'skinny account' arrangements. Lawmakers are evaluating the regulatory implications and risks of granting direct Fed access to firms in emerging financial sectors.

startups-vc.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
startups-vc.summary: Vishal Sikka, former Infosys chief, has launched a new venture backed by prominent investors Mayfield and Aramco Ventures to disrupt the IT services industry. The startup brings together seasoned executives from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the established enterprise software and services landscape.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: World-Model Startup Odyssey Leads With $310M In Slower Week For Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: World-model AI startup Odyssey led this week's funding activity with a $310 million round in what was an otherwise slower period for large venture capital deals. The week saw notable investments across diverse sectors including AI, fintech, quantum computing, biotech, and cybersecurity.

startups-vc.title: Valor Equity Partners looks to raise a $2.5B Fund VII, per Bloomberg
startups-vc.summary: Valor Equity Partners is planning to raise $2.5 billion for its latest fund as the firm continues to expand its venture capital portfolio. This announcement provides new details on a fundraising effort the firm had previously announced without specifying a target amount.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: XCures Lands $46M Series B To Clean Up Messy Medical Records With AI
startups-vc.summary: XCures, an AI-powered startup focused on streamlining patient data and medical records management, has secured a $46 million Series B round led by Innovius Capital. The funding will enable the company to expand its platform that helps healthcare organizations organize and leverage fragmented medical data.

startups-vc.title: Why Ex-Meta CTO Mike Schroepfer Says It's A Great Time To Build A Hard Tech Company: 'Infrastructure Is The Moat'
startups-vc.summary: Gigascale Capital founder Mike Schroepfer discusses the emerging power constraints and infrastructure challenges that are creating strategic advantages for hard tech companies in areas like batteries and robotics. He argues that infrastructure has become the defining competitive moat for the next wave of transformative technologies.

startups-vc.title: Slate Auto's radically simple electric truck starts at $24,950
startups-vc.summary: Slate Auto, the Bezos-backed EV startup, revealed pricing for its debut vehicles with the electric truck starting at $24,950 and the SUV variant at $29,950, while increasing base range to 205 miles. The aggressive pricing positions the startup to challenge traditional automotive manufacturers in the affordable EV market.

startups-vc.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they're the most resilient
startups-vc.summary: Despite widespread concerns that artificial intelligence would eliminate technical roles, new data from SignalFire shows that engineers represent a growing share of new hires across the startup ecosystem. The findings challenge the dominant narrative around AI-driven job displacement in the technology sector.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has announced Harshita Arora as a new General Partner, bringing deep expertise in fintech and infrastructure alongside founder experience dating back to her teenage years. Her addition strengthens YC's investment team with perspectives from building companies across multiple sectors.

startups-vc.title: Adobe Just Deferred a Big Annual Price Increase. It's the First Big Crack in B2B Pricing Power Since 2022
startups-vc.summary: Adobe's decision to defer a planned annual price increase marks a significant shift in B2B software pricing strategy, breaking a four-year trend of consistent annual increases and AI feature upsells. The move signals weakening customer lock-in and suggests that SaaS pricing power may be facing structural headwinds.

startups-vc.title: Here's why Slate changed the battery in its cheap EV truck
startups-vc.summary: Slate Auto switched battery technology for its affordable electric truck after a multi-year buildup of momentum and strategic analysis supporting the decision change. The battery swap represents an important engineering pivot in the company's effort to deliver cost-effective electric vehicles to the mass market.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor working for the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and classified agency secrets on a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and revoke the exposed credentials as lawmakers from both chambers demand accountability.

cybersecurity.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released software updates addressing nearly 200 security vulnerabilities across Windows and supported products, marking a record monthly patching cycle for the company. Nearly 36 of the flaws received critical severity ratings, and exploit code for at least three vulnerabilities is already publicly available.

cybersecurity.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers identified a critical class of CI/CD workflow vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to hijack repository workflows and compromise open-source software supply chains. The weakness, dubbed Cordyceps, affects hundreds of repositories at major organizations including Microsoft, Google, and Apache.

cybersecurity.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors have deployed a Golang-based sniffer targeting approximately 430,000 FortiGate firewalls to extract sensitive credentials in an ongoing global campaign. The attack resulted in the identification of roughly 110 million stolen credentials from compromised firewall infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors have begun exploiting CVE-2026-20230, a critical vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to write files and gain root-level access. The flaw stems from improper input validation in specific HTTP requests and carries a CVSS score of 8.6.

cybersecurity.title: 'Popa' Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
cybersecurity.summary: The Popa Android botnet, which has compromised millions of consumer TV boxes over four years to relay fraudulent internet traffic, has been attributed to NetNut, a residential proxy provider operated by publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies Ltd. The infrastructure has been used for advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data scraping operations.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
cybersecurity.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency issued a warning about active exploitation of CVE-2025-67038, a critical code injection vulnerability in Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices with a CVSS score of 9.8. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies have been ordered to apply patches by June 26, 2026.

cybersecurity.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
cybersecurity.summary: Law enforcement agencies in coordination with private sector security firms including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft successfully dismantled criminal infrastructure supporting Amadey and StealC malware. The operation disrupted the malware distribution network used to launch ransomware attacks, financial fraud schemes, and infrastructure assaults.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch authorities arrested two co-owners of internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to conduct cyberattacks, influence campaigns, and disinformation operations targeting the European Union. The investigation seized 800 servers previously controlled by the hosting firms that managed sanctioned infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
cybersecurity.summary: Additional victims have been identified following a breach of application vendor Klue, where attackers leveraged stolen OAuth tokens to access customer Salesforce data. The expanded scope of the Icarus campaign demonstrates the cascading impact of third-party software compromises on enterprise customer bases.

energy-climate.title: DOE offers $17.5B in loans to help build 10 large nuclear reactors
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. Department of Energy is offering $17.5 billion in loans to support the construction of 10 large nuclear reactors, with major utilities including Dominion Energy, DTE Energy, and Entergy positioned to benefit. This substantial federal investment represents a significant commitment to expanding nuclear capacity as part of the nation's decarbonization strategy.

energy-climate.title: Pattern Energy launches SunZia energy project in US
energy-climate.summary: Pattern Energy has announced full operational launch of the SunZia renewable energy project, a 3,650 MW wind facility representing one of the largest renewable infrastructure deployments in the United States. The project's completion marks a major milestone in expanding U.S. wind generation capacity.

energy-climate.title: Renewable investment enters a new phase as grids become the limiting factor
energy-climate.summary: Renewable energy investment is projected to exceed $700 billion in 2026, yet the industry faces a critical shift as grid infrastructure emerges as the primary constraint on continued deployment. This analysis highlights that transmission and grid integration, rather than renewable technology costs, have become the central challenge for energy transition.

energy-climate.title: Escaping the energy straitjacket
energy-climate.summary: Europe's fragmented grid infrastructure and lack of central planning prevent optimal use of renewable capacity, trapping the continent in volatile prices driven by fossil fuel imports. Consolidating Europe's national grids into a unified network could unlock significant untapped potential from solar, wind, and storage resources, according to Supergrid Europe's executive director.

energy-climate.title: Google and Energy Dome plan 200MWh CO2 battery project in Ireland
energy-climate.summary: Google and Energy Dome have announced plans for a 200 MWh commercial energy storage facility utilizing innovative CO2 battery technology in County Offaly, Ireland. The project demonstrates growing corporate investment in alternative energy storage solutions to support renewable integration.

energy-climate.title: Grid-forming BESS to enhance stability in utility-scale PV
energy-climate.summary: Researchers have demonstrated through simulation that grid-forming battery energy storage systems with advanced control strategies can provide immediate grid support to large-scale solar plants, enabling transition toward inverter-dominated power systems. The study provides quantitative evidence that a 100 MW solar facility paired with 35 MW/60 MWh storage can maintain grid stability without traditional synchronous generators.

energy-climate.title: Operations begin at 800MW Green River Energy Centre in Utah
energy-climate.summary: rPlus Energies has commenced commercial operations at the Green River Energy Center, an 800 MW facility in Emery County, Utah, adding significant generation capacity to the western U.S. energy grid. The project represents a major infrastructure investment in regional renewable and energy resources.

energy-climate.title: 3 home energy providers offer 16.8 GW of distributed capacity to utilities, hyperscalers
energy-climate.summary: Three major home energy providers are offering 16.8 GW of distributed generation capacity to utilities and hyperscalers through active distributed power plant programs. The initiative, exemplified by successful deployments in Puerto Rico, highlights the potential of aggregated residential resources as a strategic grid resource.

energy-climate.title: Turkish PV keeps growing amid policy shift, financing constraints
energy-climate.summary: Turkey's solar photovoltaic market continues to expand despite policy transitions and financing challenges, driven primarily by strong industrial demand and a competitive investment culture. The growth demonstrates the market's resilience and the structural shift toward commercial and industrial solar adoption in the country.

energy-climate.title: Platte River, EnergyHub partner to deploy 39-MW Colorado VPP
energy-climate.summary: Platte River Power Authority is partnering with EnergyHub to deploy a 39 MW virtual power plant program in Colorado, aiming to secure 19 MW from customer-owned distributed resources by 2030. The initial programs are scheduled to launch this summer, marking utilities' strategic shift toward aggregating distributed energy resources for grid management.

asia-pacific.title: Malaysian student dies after collapsing during rugby training at school
asia-pacific.summary:
A Form Two male student died after collapsing during a rugby training session at a secondary school in Telok Mas, Malaysia’s Melaka state.
The 14-year-old is believed to have lost consciousness at about 6pm on Wednesday shortly after a break while listening to a briefing by his coach.
The victim’s granduncle, Yahya Endut, 64, said he rushed from Alor Gajah after receiving news of the incident involving the teenager, whom he had raised since childhood.
“I was informed that the victim had only...

asia-pacific.title: Power of Hong Kong’s new national security regulation must be exercised prudently
asia-pacific.summary:
The recent enactment of the Safeguarding National Security (Procedural Matters) Regulation has generated significant interest and commentary on Hong Kong’s legal landscape.
It is recognised that maintaining national security is of paramount importance and that there is a constitutional responsibility on the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region to do so.
Under the Basic Law, that responsibility falls to a large extent on the city’s chief executive. It is also recognised in many common law...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong can take the regulatory high road amid US-China AI decoupling
asia-pacific.summary:
The market realignment over the past week starkly captures the self-defeating logic of America’s technology containment strategy.
The momentum began on June 15, when the shares of Chinese artificial intelligence pioneer Zhipu AI, which trade in Hong Kong under Knowledge Atlas Technology, surged by 48 per cent intraday after it announced the open-source release of its GLM-5.2 foundation model. The rally continued this week, with Zhipu’s shares trading at an intraday high of HK$2,980 (US$380) in..

asia-pacific.title: Chinese boy destroys US$30,000 worth of phones in home fire; dad’s response shocks everyone
asia-pacific.summary:
A boy in southern China accidentally ignited dozens of mobile phones while playing at home, but his father’s remarkably calm reaction has garnered praise online.
The father, surnamed Peng, operates a mobile phone shop in Shenzhen and utilises part of his home as a workshop for storing devices, according to the mainland media outlet First Scene.
The fire began when Peng’s five-year-old son noticed sparks from a short-circuiting power strip and, intrigued, used them to ignite a tissue.
The flames.

asia-pacific.title: ‘No limits’ to accountability over Tai Po fire, security chief Chris Tang says
asia-pacific.summary:
As Hong Kong marks the 29th anniversary of its return to Chinese rule on July 1, the South China Morning Post talks to the city’s senior officials about the administration’s achievements so far and what may lie ahead.
Authorities will arrest and prosecute anyone if there is evidence of their wrongdoing in the Tai Po fire tragedy to ensure justice is served without needing the independent committee to become a legal commission of inquiry, Hong Kong’s security chief has vowed.
In an exclusive...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong Science Fair marks five years of youth innovation
asia-pacific.summary:
[The content of this article has been produced by our advertising partner.]
For five years in a row the Hong Kong Science Fair has worked to build up the talent pipeline that will support scientific discovery and innovation in Hong Kong and across the country. 
By sparking ideas in children and instilling an entrepreneurial spirit in their formative years, it helps turn young curiosity into real-world solutions that support scientific discovery and innovation. 
The government is fully committed.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong prime office vacancy hits 7-month low on Central spillover demand
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s overall prime office vacancy rate fell to a seven-month low in May, driven by spillover demand from Central, where premium offices were close to full occupancy, according to JLL.
With Central’s grade A office vacancy rate holding steady at 9.2 per cent, tenants appeared to have found their way to neighbouring Wan Chai and Causeway Bay. Vacancy there edged down to 9.8 per cent in May from 10.4 per cent in April, the lowest in 10 months, the property consultancy said in its latest...

asia-pacific.title: Powerful earthquakes rock Venezuela, bringing down buildings in Caracas
asia-pacific.summary:
⁠Strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela’s capital ⁠on Wednesday afternoon, bringing down buildings in Caracas and causing scientists to estimate “high casualties and extensive damage” to the South American country.
A magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck about 160km (100 miles) west of Caracas, with a magnitude 7.5 tremor hitting less than a minute later, ‌according to the US Geological Survey (USGS).
“High casualties and extensive damage are probable and the disaster is likely widespread,” the..

asia-pacific.title: General who was last US soldier out of Afghanistan suddenly steps down
asia-pacific.summary:
The US Army’s commander of its forces in Europe and Africa – who was famously the last American soldier to leave Afghanistan in 2021 – is unexpectedly stepping down from his post after just 18 months in the job, the Army confirmed late on Tuesday.
General Christopher Donahue, commanding general of US Army Europe and Africa and commander of Nato’s Allied Land Command, will relinquish his command on July 2, according to an Army statement.
He is the latest in a line of nearly two dozen top military

asia-pacific.title: Keiko Fujimori vows to unite a Peru ‘split in two’ as run-off lead holds
asia-pacific.summary:
Right-wing ⁠candidate Keiko Fujimori said on ⁠Wednesday she would seek to ⁠unite a Peru “split in two” if she takes office, after razor-thin election results gave her enough votes to secure what appears to be an insurmountable lead in the presidential run-off.
Fujimori, a four-time presidential hopeful and the daughter of late former president Alberto Fujimori, said ‌she would make an “open call” to experienced technocrats to form her first cabinet, as she seeks to fulfil campaign promises to...
